footer.tagline: Predictions with receipts. Calibrated crypto forecasts across every chain, token, and timeframe.

meta.title: Predict — Vizzor
meta.description: Ask Vizzor for a calibrated crypto prediction. Three free predictions per browser. Powered by the same engine as the Telegram bot.

predict.title: Predictions, on the page.
predict.sub: Type a symbol and a horizon — say "ETH 4h" or "SOL 1d" — and get the same calibrated, receipt-backed prediction the Telegram bot produces. First three are on the house.
predict.emptyState: Ask for a prediction ("BTC 4h", "ETH 1d", "SOL 15m") or type /help for commands.

freeTier.label: Free predictions
freeTier.body: Used {used} of {limit}. Resets after the $VIZZOR token launches, when each additional prediction costs a small token burn.

waitlist.label: $VIZZOR · launching soon
waitlist.title: Free tier exhausted.
waitlist.body: The paid path activates when $VIZZOR launches — each additional prediction will burn a small amount of token. In the meantime, the Telegram bot has no per-user cap.

paid.label: $VIZZOR · paid mode
paid.title: Free tier exhausted.
paid.body: Connect a Solana wallet and burn {amount} $VIZZOR per prediction. Each burn is verified on-chain before the next prediction streams.
paid.legal: Burns are final. Verify the network and amount before signing.

wallet.mintNotConfigured: Mint address not configured. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_VIZZOR_MINT to enable burns.

exhaustedBanner.label: Free tier exhausted
exhaustedBanner.body: You've used all three free predictions. The Telegram bot has no per-user cap, or wait for the $VIZZOR token launch to continue here.

welcome.title: What should Vizzor predict?
welcome.sub: Type a symbol and horizon — "BTC 4h" — or run a tool like /wr, /precisions, /price. Three free predictions per browser.

composer.footer: Vizzor can be wrong. Verify on-chain. Not financial advice.

hero.headline: Predictions with receipts.
hero.sub: AI-powered crypto forecasts across every chain and timeframe. Numbers, not opinions.

whatsInIt.title: One prediction. Full receipts.
whatsInIt.sub: Ask Vizzor about any token. You get a directional call, target prices, signal breakdown, and an auto-armed alert at the level the engine says matters.

bullets.targets: Bull / base / bear targets at the requested horizon
bullets.snapshot: Trigger snapshot — every signal that fired, no hidden contributors

howItWorks.title: Six perspectives. One honest call.
howItWorks.lede: Vizzor reads the market from six different angles at once. When most of them agree, you get a prediction. When they don't, you stay quiet.

fetch.title: Listen
fetch.description: Twelve live data feeds — prices on every exchange, whale wallets moving on-chain, breaking news, what people are betting on prediction markets. Refreshed every few seconds.
fetch.ariaLabel: Six input signals fanning into a single fusion node

fuse.title: Cross-check
fuse.description: Each feed votes. Strong signals carry weight; weak ones get dampened. The engine learns from every past call — hits raise their family's vote, misses dampen it.
fuse.ariaLabel: Two converging curves meeting at a pulsing intersection point

emit.title: Wait for an A+ setup
emit.description: Most of the time, the signals disagree — and Vizzor stays silent. Only when at least four of six lenses line up does a prediction get sent. Quality over volume.
emit.ariaLabel: Four gates of increasing height with a star marking the final A+ emission

liveReceipts.title: Calls in, results out.
liveReceipts.lede: Every prediction gets tracked, resolved, and graded. The decisive WR you see at the top is what's left when we strip out the marketing.
liveReceipts.footerNote: Showing the most recent 10. Full history with filters →

surfaceCompare.title: One engine. Three surfaces.
surfaceCompare.lede: Vizzor doesn't lock you into one place. Pick the surface that matches how you live — they all share the same predictions and the same trigger snapshot.

telegram.title: Telegram bot
telegram.sub: Zero install. 32 commands. Where Vizzor lives day-to-day.

features.alerts: Auto-armed price alerts on every trade plan

cli.title: Command line
cli.sub: Indie quant chic. 18 commands. Pipes cleanly into your terminal workflow.

features.offline: Local SQLite state · runs offline with Ollama

dashboard.title: Web dashboard
dashboard.sub: Visual. Real-time. For when you want to look at the receipts, not just read them.

sixSignals.title: Six independent lenses. One verdict.
sixSignals.lede: No single signal carries a prediction. Each family must contribute — and signals that disagree get a penalty, not a vote.

onChain.description: Whale flows, smart-money accumulation, stablecoin mint/burn velocity, exchange-flow imbalance, Coinbase premium.
onChain.reveal: Multi-chain, free-tier APIs only. $250k+ events on 30s cadence.

mlEnsemble.description: Python sidecar (XGBoost + LightGBM + isotonic) OR JS-native fallback (RSI/MACD/Bollinger/EMA/volume/funding).
mlEnsemble.reveal: Zero silent degradation when Python is absent.

predictionMarkets.description: Gamma + Polymarket implied probabilities with edge detection.
predictionMarkets.reveal: Trades the spread when ChronoVisor disagrees with the market.

socialNarrative.description: News NLP (CryptoPanic + CoinGecko) + LLM catalyst classifier (prompt-cached, budget-gated).
socialNarrative.reveal: Headlines normalized to a calibrated sentiment CF, not a vibe score.

patternMatch.description: Smart Money Concepts (BOS / CHoCH / liquidity sweeps) + ICT timing (kill-zones).
patternMatch.reveal: Pattern library auto-populated from resolved backtest.

logicRules.description: 14 base + 7 scalping FOL rules over derivatives, macro, and on-chain features.
logicRules.reveal: Per-rule accuracy tracked; poor rules halve their CF, good rules get +20%.

trustBecauseTracked.title: We track our own win rate. Decisively.

highConviction.name: high-conviction
highConviction.description: ≥4-of-6 signal agreement + composite confidence ≥75 + horizon proven via calibrator.

whaleConfirmed.name: whale-confirmed
whaleConfirmed.description: High-conviction AND whale flow confirms direction. Built on multi-chain free-tier flow analysis.

tracked.name: tracked
tracked.description: Passes all gates but doesn't meet the higher bars. Still counted in the strict WR cohort.

advisory.name: advisory
advisory.description: Passes only the soft gates. Excluded from the strict cohort but still resolves and feeds the calibrator.

ctaBlock.title: Predictions you can actually grade.
ctaBlock.body: Open the Telegram bot, ask for a prediction on any token, and watch it resolve. If it misses, the next one carries that into the calibrator. Receipts compound.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.sub: Three tiers, two billing cadences, six chains accepted. Cancel anytime. Self-host if you'd rather not pay a subscription at all.

free.name: Free
free.sub: Get started with Vizzor on Telegram

pro.name: Pro
pro.sub: Unlimited signals for the active manual trader

features.allTiers: All tier badges (✅ tracked + 🐋 whale-confirmed + 🌟 high-conviction)
features.alerts: Unlimited /alerts · TP/SL price thresholds armed automatically
features.polymarketAlerts: Polymarket edge alerts when ChronoVisor disagrees with the market
features.priority: Priority during overload — skip the queue

elite.name: Elite
elite.sub: Autonomous on-chain trading agents acting on Vizzor signals

features.agents: 🤖 Autonomous trading agents — execute trades on-chain the moment a signal fires, no manual click
features.agentWallets: Per-agent encrypted multi-chain wallets · isolated risk, hot-key never leaves the device
features.polymarketAgent: Polymarket trading agent — places trades when ChronoVisor odds beat the market
features.circuitBreaker: Per-agent loss limits + global /emergency-stop — instant kill across every running agent
features.restApi: REST API (60 req/min) — query agents, force trades, override decisions programmatically
features.privateChannel: Private Telegram + Discord channels with the engineering team
features.headStart: 5-minute head start on every signal emission + early access to new agents and signals

trial.label: 7-day trial
trial.title: Every new user gets Pro for 7 days
trial.body: On your first /start, full Pro access activates automatically. After 7 days the bot auto-downgrades to Free — no card on file, no expiry surprise, no charge.

chains.title: Six chains accepted
chains.sub: TON Connect for instant confirms on Phase 1. Self-hosted EVM, Solana, and TRON watchers for stablecoin payments — no middleman, no Stripe, no card data.

trial.q: How does the 7-day Pro trial work?
trial.a: Send /start to the bot. Full Pro access is granted automatically — unlimited predictions, every tier badge, /alerts. After 7 days the bot auto-downgrades to Free. No card on file, nothing to cancel.

cancel.a: Yes. Subscriptions are per-period (monthly or annual). When the current period ends, the bot downgrades you to Free. There's no card on file to charge — you pay each period with a fresh on-chain transaction. Stop paying = automatic downgrade.

chains.q: Which chains can I pay from?
chains.a: Phase 1 (live now): TON native via TON Connect. Phase 2: USDC on Polygon, Base, Arbitrum, Solana, and USDT on TRON. Each payment is verified by a self-hosted watcher on the bot — no Stripe, no Plaid, no card data, no middleman.

selfHost.a: Yes. The CLI, Telegram bot, ChronoVisor engine, and dashboard ship under BUSL-1.1. Clone github.com/7ayLabs/vizzor, run `docker compose up -d`, and you have the same engine the hosted bot uses — no subscription required.

difference.q: What's the practical difference between Free and Pro?
difference.a: Free shows you Vizzor's calibrated baseline (✅ tracked + ⚪ advisory tiers). Pro unlocks 🐋 whale-confirmed and 🌟 high-conviction tiers — the high-WR cohort that drives most of the alpha. If you're using Vizzor seriously, Pro pays for itself in one good signal.

refund.a: Within 48 hours of payment, message the bot with /refund and we'll send back the on-chain amount (minus network fees). After 48 hours, no refunds — but you can still cancel for the next period at any time.

selfHost.title: Or run the engine yourself
selfHost.sub: Vizzor is BUSL-1.1 licensed. Self-host the bot, point /start at your own Telegram instance, and skip the subscription entirely. The math is identical, the receipts are identical, your data stays on your VPS.

meta.title: Checkout · {tier} {cadence} · Vizzor

pay.title: Activate {tier}

pay.legalFootnote: On-chain payment · 5-min rate lock · cancel anytime

error.body: Something went wrong. You can retry.

error.featureDisabled: Payments aren't accepted on this chain yet. Try the other Phase-1 option, or open the Telegram bot.
error.engineOffline: The Vizzor payment engine is temporarily unreachable. The site is online but can't create a session right now. Retry in a moment.
error.engineError: Payment infrastructure pending: the Vizzor engine doesn't have payment endpoints deployed yet. Use the Telegram bot to pay for now — same tier, same price.
error.engineMarkedFailed: The engine rejected this session. Retry, or contact support if it persists.
error.rateUnavailable: We couldn't lock the exchange rate. Retry in a moment.
error.invalidInput: The request shape we sent the server isn't valid. Refresh the page and pick a tier from /pricing.
error.unsupportedChain: This chain isn't supported yet. Switch to TON or $VIZZOR on Solana.
error.priceLookup: Couldn't look up the tier price on the server. Refresh the page.
error.sessionFailed: Session creation failed. Retry, or open the Telegram bot if it persists.
error.mintNotConfigured: The $VIZZOR mint address isn't configured on this deploy. Switch to TON, or wait for the launch.
error.walletRejected: You rejected the transaction in your wallet. Click Pay again when you're ready.
error.unknown: Something went wrong. Retry, or use the Telegram bot if the problem persists.

pending.label: payment infrastructure pending
pending.title: Crypto checkout launches soon
pending.body: The on-site TON Connect flow is built but waiting on the engine's payment endpoints to land. In the meantime, you can pay directly through the Telegram bot — same tier, same price, same access.

grant.label: subscription unlocked
grant.title: Welcome to Vizzor · redeem in Telegram
grant.body: Click the button below to open the Telegram bot. The bot reads the grant code embedded in the deep-link and activates your subscription immediately.

success.pendingBody: We're still waiting for the watcher to mint your grant code. Hold tight — usually a few seconds.
success.missingBody: The session id is missing or unknown. Start a new payment from the pricing page.

manifesto.manifestoLede: Vizzor exists because the crypto information layer is broken. Not bearish — broken. We're trying to repair one specific part of it.

section1.title: Most crypto AI is a chat box with a price feed.
section1.body:
The pattern is everywhere. Take a language model, give it the BTC price, wrap it in a chat interface, call it an AI trader. The output reads like analysis. It is not analysis. It is a fluent paraphrase of whatever happened to be near the model's context window.

Fluent paraphrase is dangerous in markets because the surface looks like a quantitative tool and the substance is a vibe. When the vibe is wrong, the user has no way to know why — there are no signals, no votes, no resolved cohort, nothing to grade. Just a confident sentence that didn't pan out.

Vizzor is the opposite construction. Six independent signal families do the work; the language model only renders the result. If the engine has nothing to say, the language model says nothing. Silence is a feature.

section2.title: Calibrated confidence is a feature.
section2.body:
Every prediction carries a confidence number that has been Platt-scaled against the resolved cohort. When Vizzor says 72%, it means that across the last several hundred 72%-confidence calls, roughly 72% landed in band. Not metaphor — calibration.

That number does work. It tells you when to size up, when to size down, and when to ignore the call entirely. It is also how the engine catches itself drifting: if the calibrated 72% bucket starts hitting at 58%, the calibrator notices and pulls the curve. Models that don't track their own calibration cannot do this.

section3.title: The receipts are the product.
section3.body:
Every prediction the engine ever emitted lives in `chronovisor_predictions`, with its full trigger snapshot — which signals voted, which direction, what CF, why it passed the gates. The resolved outcome is grafted on when the horizon expires.

That archive is public. The win rate on the home page is computed from that archive. Filter by horizon, filter by tier, filter by chain — the WR you see is the WR you'd compute yourself.

This is the part of the product that earns the rest. A prediction engine that hides its history is not a prediction engine, it is a marketing surface for a forecast that has not been tested.

section4.title: Free-tier APIs are the moat, not the bottleneck.
section4.body:
We are sometimes asked why Vizzor doesn't integrate Nansen, Arkham, or one of the paid intelligence stacks. The answer is that the moat is in the synthesis, not the data. Anyone willing to pay can buy the same Nansen feed.

Vizzor reads whale flow, smart-money accumulation, exchange premia, and stablecoin velocity from chain RPCs and free-tier endpoints. The interesting work is fusing six families through certainty-factor algebra with weak-signal dampening, Bayesian update with family-aware priors, and Platt calibration against the resolved cohort.

That work travels. It runs on a $5 VPS or a laptop. It does not get more interesting when you bolt on a $1,200/month feed.

section5.title: Operators run Vizzor. We just maintain it.
section5.body:
The hosted Telegram bot is convenient. It is also a courtesy, not the product. The product is the open-source engine, the configurable thresholds, the CLI you can pipe into your own shell scripts, the docker-compose file that brings up an entire local stack.

We will keep hosting the bot as long as the bill stays sane. If it ever doesn't, the project keeps working — every operator already has the code, the config, and the data schema. Vizzor was built to outlast its hosting.
